name: nextflow
tagline: Reference skill for building, running, and debugging Nextflow/nf-core bioinformatics pipelines, plus CLI and config guidance.
verdict: Comprehensive, well-scoped, and honestly written skill with no manipulation or hidden instructions. Main risk: the Setup section has the user run `curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash` then `sudo mv` the result onto PATH — a curl-pipe-to-shell plus sudo install with no checksum shown, a real (if commonly used) supply-chain pattern users installing this ski

highlights.summary: Setup section line 38: `curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash` pipes a remote script directly into bash with no integrity check shown. It mirrors Nextflow's own official install docs, but is still a

Line 39: `sudo mv nextflow /usr/local/bin/` requires elevated privileges during install. Standard for this tool's official install flow, but worth flagging as a privilege-escalation step a user should

Install paths include a curl|bash script from get.nextflow.io and `pip install nf-core` / conda installs from bioconda/conda-forge — mainstream but unpinned/unverified sources (no hash/signature check
